preamble: The shift from the voiced postalveolar fricative [ʒ] to the voiced velar fricative [ɣ] is the voiced counterpart to the [ʃ] to [x] shift, representing a systematic backing of sibilants.

examples.note: The voiced postalveolar fricative [ʒ] retracted and eventually merged with the voiceless velar fricative after devoicing.

sources: Penny, R. (2002). A History of the Spanish Language.
Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
